name: Uniform Composable DXP Orchestration API
what_it_does: Composable DXP orchestration platform with REST API — enabling enterprises to visually compose experiences from multiple headless content sources (Contentful, Sanity, Sitecore, Salesforce, etc.), personalize and A/B test without code changes, manage omnichannel content delivery, and give marketing teams a visual editor that aggregates content from their existing tools.
use_cases: Orchestrating content from multiple headless CMS sources (Contentful, Sanity, Sitecore) via Uniform API
Running A/B tests and personalizing experiences via Uniform without code deploys
Delivering composable page data via Uniform API for Next.js/Nuxt headless frontend
Enabling marketing teams to visually assemble pages from multi-source components via Uniform Canvas
Managing omnichannel experience delivery across web, mobile, and digital signage via Uniform API
not_for: Teams needing a single CMS (Uniform is for orchestrating multiple existing CMSs, not replacing them)
Simple single-source headless CMS (use Contentful or Sanity directly for single-source)
Small teams without existing composable tech stack investment
best_when: A large enterprise has already invested in multiple best-of-breed tools (Contentful for content, Shopify for commerce, Algolia for search) and needs a visual orchestration layer that lets marketing compose experiences from these sources without engineering involvement.
avoid_when: You're consolidating on a single CMS, have a small/mid-market stack, or need a CMS from scratch rather than orchestration.

auth.notes: API key from Uniform workspace. Bearer token auth. Project-scoped access. Webhook for composition and content change events. REST API for composition (canvas) data delivery. SDK (@uniformdev/canvas, @uniformdev/canvas-react) for visual editor integration. Connectors for Contentful, Sanity, Sitecore, BigCommerce, Shopify, Algolia, and 50+ services. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free trial available; no free tier
pricing.paid_tiers: Starter from $5,000/year; Growth from $25,000/year; Enterprise custom; contact sales
pricing.notes: Austin, Texas company. Founded 2020 by Lars Petersen (former Sitecore CTO) and Adam Singer. Raised $18M Series A (2022). Backed by Insight Partners, Bessemer Venture Partners. Built by Sitecore veterans who saw the market moving to composable. 'MACH Alliance' founding member (Microservices, API-first, Cloud-native, Headless). Strong in large enterprise already invested in composable stack. Customers include Lufthansa, Boohoo, L'Oreal. Composable DXP is the Gartner term for combining best-of-breed headless tools.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for composition data. API key auth. Good developer documentation with connector guides. Webhook for composition events. SDK required for visual editing (Canvas). Requires existing headless CMS and other composable tools as content sources. Annual contract required. Built by Sitecore veterans — enterprise-quality architecture.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Composition reads fully idempotent. Canvas data reads idempotent by route. Component reads idempotent by ID. Personalization variant reads idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Uniform orchestrates multiple CMSs — each source CMS also needs to be configured and authenticated
Annual contract required — significant investment for orchestration layer
SDK required for visual Canvas editing — REST API alone provides composition data only
Composable DXP requires existing investment in headless tools (Contentful, Shopify, etc.)
Personalization requires Uniform Track (analytics) configuration for visitor segmentation

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 and GDPR compliant. US and EU data residency. API key auth. Insight Partners/Bessemer backing with enterprise security expectations. Multiple CMS source credentials stored in Uniform — strict secret management required. Personalization data (visitor segments, A/B test assignments) is privacy-sensitive — GDPR compliance needed. Each content source connection must be secured separately. API key provides full project access.
